# UC-22.11.5 · DMZ Originated Sessions Hitting CDE Internal Segments

## Description

Detects allowed paths from DMZ assets into internal CDE networks that violate one-way DMZ design, reducing the chance a compromised web tier pivots into systems that store or process account data.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes netfw and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Refresh DMZ and CDE internal inventories weekly from CMDB. (2) Validate expected admin SSH paths. (3) Create ES notable for sustained spikes. (4) Document compensating controls if architecture requires exceptions. Indexes required: `index=netfw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`; Lookups: ``pci_dmz_assets.csv`, `pci_cde_internal`, `pci_cde_internal.csv`, `pci_dmz_assets`, `pci_dmz_assets.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`; asset lookup `pci_dmz_assets.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Refresh DMZ and CDE internal inventories weekly from CMDB. (2) Validate expected admin SSH paths. (3) Create ES notable for sustained spikes. (4) Document compensating controls if architecture requires exceptions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_dmz_assets.csv ip AS src OUTPUT in_dmz
| lookup pci_cde_internal.csv ip AS dest OUTPUT in_cde_internal
| where in_dmz="true" AND in_cde_internal="true"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, src, dest, app, dest_port
| eventstats perc95(count) as p95 globally
| where count>p95 AND p95>0
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**DMZ Originated Sessions Hitting CDE Internal Segments (PCI DSS Req 1.3.4, 1.3.7)** — Detects allowed paths from DMZ assets into internal CDE networks that violate one-way DMZ design, reducing the chance a compromised web tier pivots into systems that store or process account data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`; asset lookup `pci_dmz_assets.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: netfw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=netfw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where in_dmz="true" AND in_cde_internal="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, src, dest, app, dest_port** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>p95 AND p95>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.app, All_Traffic.dest_port span=1h | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**DMZ Originated Sessions Hitting CDE Internal Segments (PCI DSS Req 1.3.4, 1.3.7)** — Detects allowed paths from DMZ assets into internal CDE networks that violate one-way DMZ design, reducing the chance a compromised web tier pivots into systems that store or process account data.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`; asset lookup `pci_dmz_assets.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (hourly counts), bubble chart (src size by count), table (spike windows).

## SPL

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_dmz_assets.csv ip AS src OUTPUT in_dmz
| lookup pci_cde_internal.csv ip AS dest OUTPUT in_cde_internal
| where in_dmz="true" AND in_cde_internal="true"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, src, dest, app, dest_port
| eventstats perc95(count) as p95 globally
| where count>p95 AND p95>0
| sort - count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Line chart (hourly counts), bubble chart (src size by count), table (spike windows).
